briefdescription.para: True random seed source enable mask.
Below registers are not directly referenced in driver but are accessed accessed with offset from TRNG_EXT_SEED_0.

detaileddescription.para: Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_1 0x00000044U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_2 0x00000048U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_3 0x0000004CU Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_4 0x00000050U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_5 0x00000054U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_6 0x00000058U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_7 0x0000005CU Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_8 0x00000060U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_9 0x00000064U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_10 0x00000068U Register: TRNG_EXT_SEED_11 0x0000006CU- TRNG personalization string 0 offset

briefdescription.para: Below registers are not directly referenced in driver but are accessed accessed with offset from TRNG_PER_STRNG_0.

detaileddescription.para: Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_1 0x00000084U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_2 0x00000088U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_3 0x0000008CU Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_4 0x00000090U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_5 0x00000094U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_6 0x00000098U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_7 0x0000009CU Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_8 0x000000A0U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_9 0x000000A4U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_10 0x000000A8U Register: TRNG_PER_STRNG_11 0x000000ACU- TRNG core output offset

sectiondef.header: Constant definitions for parameters used for Health and KAT tests

briefdescription.para: Constant definitions for Health and KAT tests.
0x11 - Invalid driver state for requested operation
0x21 - Invalid TRNG mode in user configuration
0x22 - Invalid seed life value in user configuration
0x23 - Invalid prediction resistance setting in user configuration
0x24 - No seed provided for instantiation in DRNG mode
0x26 - Invalid DF length multiplier in user configuration
0x30 - No seed provided for reseed operation
0x31 - Seed operation not valid for current mode
0x32 - Same seed used as previous reseed
0x33 - Invalid DF length multiplier for reseed
0x35 - Software A5 pattern check failed during certification test
0x43 - Reseed required due to prediction resistance
0x44 - Invalid prediction resistance value for generate
0x50 - Error copying data for derivative function
0x51 - Failed to setup key for derivative function
0x52 - Memory set error in derivative function
0x53 - Memory move error in derivative function
0x62 - Error copying user configuration during KAT
0 - Driver is not in Initialized state
1 - Driver is not in Healthy state
This API is used to reset the TRNG.

detaileddescription.para: There are two resets involved (RESET register and TRNG_CTRL.PRNGsrst), both of which are asserted and de-asserted again after a delay.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This API is used to issue soft reset the TRNG (TRNG_CTRL.PRNGsrst)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This API is used to keep the TRNG in reset state.

detaileddescription.para: There are two resets involved (RESET register and TRNG_CTRL.PRNGsrst), both of which are asserted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This is the function in which actually generation of random data is started and collected random data is returned to calling function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the address of the buffer in to which the random data generated has to be copied.
is number of bytes to be collected.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Random number collection was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GENERATE_TIMEOUT if timeout occurred waiting for QCNT to become 4.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_CATASTROPHIC_DTF if DTF bit asserted in STATUS register.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_CATASTROPHIC_DTF_SW if DTF error detected in software.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GLITCH if error caused due to glitch conditions.

simplesect.para: In case of entropy data collection mode this function utilizes the additional 4 words of EntropyData buffer when NumOfRandBytes is not 32byte aligned.

listitem.para: Calculate number of generates
Read TRNG_CTRL register and update it and start the generate
De-assert PRNGMODE when prediction resistance is TRUE
Loop as many times based on NumOfRandBytes requested. In each burst 256 bits are generated
Check DTF flag
DTF flag set during generate indicates catastrophic condition, which needs to be checked for every time
Check for SW DTF error
Change endianness and store generated random data
De-assert the PRNGStart and PRNGxs bits when prediction resistance is true

briefdescription.para: This is the actual reseed function.

detaileddescription.para: This function will be called by XTrng_Reseed and during Generate operation. Instantiation too calls this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the byte array containing external seed. This shall be NULL for HRNG case.
points to Personalization string (which is recommended) in addition to seed. This is needed only during Instantiation.

is DF Length multiplier used to determine number of bits on the input of the DF construct which is (DFLenMul +1)*128 bits. This indicates the size of the seed pointed by ExtSeedPtr. This shall be 0 for Non-DF case.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Reseed was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_CERTF_SW_A5_PATTERN if entropy source outputs all 5s or all As.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_CPY_RESEED if error encountered during seed copy.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_RESEED_TIMEOUT if timeout occurred waiting for done of reseeding.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_CERTF if CTF flag is set in STATUS register.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GLITCH if error caused due to glitch conditions.
Other error codes from the called functions as defined in XTrngpsv_ErrorCodes.
Determine actual size (in bytes) of seed based on DF mode or non-DF mode

Versal TRNG IP doesn't recognize alternate 1 and 0 pattern, hence the entropy output need to be monitored before using it as seed. This means, TRNG couldn't be configured for entropy source as seed source. Instead, entropy data is collected as random data, and after inspecting for pattern, is fed again to the external seed registers. This is essentially similar to HRNG + DF case except that there is no DF involved. This actually is configuration for PTRNG mode (not for reseed) to collect random output data from entropy source.
Provide soft reset before configuring the entropy mode
TRNG_CTRL.PRNGstart will be asserted in XTrngpsv_CollectRandData()
For MISRA-C
Program TRNG_EXT_SEED and TRNG_PER_STRNG registers based on the parameters passed.
Loading personalization is used in both DRNG and HRNG cases
DF Mode
Fill the DFInput datastructure with 0s and copy the external seed (for DRNG) or collect the entropy output data to the input to the DF operation.
This actually is configuration for PTRNG mode (not for reseed) to collect random output data from entropy source.
Provide soft reset before configuring the entropy mode
TRNG_CTRL.PRNGstart will be asserted in XTrngpsv_CollectRandData()
For MISRA-C
Call the DF operation with input as external seed OR random data generated from entropy output
Output of DF (new seed) is input to the external seed registers, also configure for the external seed as seed source type.
Note that there is no personalization string programmed here as the personalization string is considered already as input to the DF.
Select Reseed operation, and configure for external seed
Start the reseed operation with above configuration and wait for STATUS.Done bit to be set. Monitor STATUS.CERTF bit, if set indicates SP800-90B entropy health test has failed.
De-assert PRNGstart bit after done with reseeding. This is required in the cases where there are two successive reseed operations are done (one in Instantiate and then in Reseed).

memberdef.argsstring: (const XTrngpsv *InstancePtr, u32 StartRegOffset, u32 NumRegs, const u8 *InitBuf)
memberdef.name: XTrngpsv_WriteRegs

detaileddescription.para: Write a byte array InitBuf as a sequence of words into an array of register locations starting with StartRegOffset. The first byte from InitBuf is stored in last byte of target register, the second byte to last but one byte of target register.. and so on. If InitBuf is NULL, the target registers are filled with all 0s.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on
contains the offset from the base address of the device from where register writes to be done
is number of registers to be programmed
is the source of buffer from where data will be written to registers.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Write to Registers is successful
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GLITCH if write and read values are different
XTRNGPSV_FAILURE if any other failure

briefdescription.para: Checks the value of seed against certain patterns, which are invalid.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the data from where data has to be checked against.
is number of bytes for which patterns has to be checked.
contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the base address of the device.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the base address of the device.
is the value to be written to the register.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 RegMask, u32 RegValue)
memberdef.name: XTrngpsv_RMW32

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the base address of the device.
indicates the bits to be modified.
is the value to be written to the register.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddr, u32 RegOffset, u32 EventMask, u32 Event, u32 Timeout)
memberdef.name: XTrngpsv_WaitForEvent

briefdescription.para: Waits for an event to occur for a certain timeout duration.

parameterdescription.para: is address of register to be checked for event(s) occurrence.
is offset of register from base address.
is mask indicating event(s) to be checked.
is specific event(s) value to be checked.
is max number of microseconds to wait for an event(s).

simplesect.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS - On occurrence of the event(s). XTRNGPSV_FAILURE - Event did not occur before counter reaches 0.

briefdescription.para: This function changes the endianness of each word in source buffer.

parameterdescription.para: points to the address of the buffer in which random data is stored.

briefdescription.para: This function detects the back to back same burst pattern and stores the last burst in to the RandBitBuf buffer so that it can be compared with next generated burst.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the address of the buffer in to which the random data generated has to be copied.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Random pattern check was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_CATASTROPHIC_DTF_SW if DTF error detected in software.
Compare previous Burst filled in last generate with current Burst
Compare bursts on current generated data
Fill current 128-bit burst in RandBitBuf

briefdescription.para: This function waits for 4 words of data to be available in core output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if event occurred.
XTRNGPSV_FAILURE if timeout occurred.

briefdescription.para: This function waits and collects 256-bit random data from core output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the address of the buffer in to which the random data generated has to be copied.
contains the value that needs to be written in to TRNG_CTRL register i.e After collection of 256-bit data it will de-assert the generate bit when prediction resistance is enabled.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if random data is copied successfully.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GENERATE_TIMEOUT if timeout occurred waiting for QCNT to become 4.
Wait for 4 words

TIME CRITICAL: When prediction resistance is enabled, it is required to stop generate operation before the completion of the update phase of the generation. The below write performs this operation. Its time critical operation and to achieve that 32-bit random data reading is unrolled instead of for loop.
Read 128-bit Burst
Wait for 4 words
De-assert PRNGMODE when prediction resistance is TRUE
Read 128-bit Burst

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the TRNGPSV instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the driver. Initialization includes only setting up the device id and base address. All the remaining parameters of the Instance will be initialized in Instantiate function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the TRNGPSV driver.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter passed to this function.
Validate arguments.
Populate Config parameters

detaileddescription.para: instantiates the TRNGPSV instance/driver with user configuration, resets the TRNG core and reseeds with initial seed and personalization string provided during Instantiation. Switching to a new state of attributes or changing any attributes (configured during Instantiation) requires UnInstantiation of the current instance and instantiation of a new instance. After successful Instantiation, State of the driver is changed to Healthy.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the user configuration structure associated with the TRNGPSV driver.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Instantiation was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter(s) passed to this function.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_NOT_UNINSTANTIATED if the driver is not uninstantiated earlier.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_MODE if invalid Mode parameter.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_SEEDLIFE if SeedLife parameter is invalid
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_PREDRES if invalid Prediction Resistance parameter.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_PERSPRES if PersStrPresent parameter is invalid.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_SEEDPRES if InitSeedPresent parameter is invalid.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_NO_SEED_INSTANTIATE if no seed passed for DRNG mode.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_UNNECESSARY_PARAM_INSTANTIATE if seed passed for HRNG mode or if unnecessary seed related parameter passed for PTRNG mode.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_DFDIS if DFDisable parameter is invalid.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_USRCFG_DFLENMUL if DFLenMul parameter is invalid.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_USRCFG_CPY if error during copy of ConfigurValues structure.
Other error codes from the called functions as defined in XTrngpsv_ErrorCodes.
Validate arguments.
If device not in uninitialized state, return error so that user can uninstantiate first before instantiating again.
Validate User configuration parameters
Copy user configuration attributes to the Instance pointer
Reset the device for a clean state
Reseed device with initial seed and personalization string
Mark the state of the device as Healthy

briefdescription.para: This function reseeds the TRNG in DRNG, HRNG modes.

detaileddescription.para: This function will be called by random number consuming application. This is just a wrapper function calling XTrng_ReseedInternal() which passes NULL as personalization string.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the byte array containing external seed. This shall be NULL for HRNG case.

is DF Length multiplier used to determine number of bits on the input of the DF construct which is (DFLenMul + 1)*128 bits. This indicates the size of the seed pointed by ExtSeedPtr. This shall be 0 for Non-DF case.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Reseed was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter(s) passed to this function.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_STATE if driver is not Healthy state before invoking this.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_SEED_INVALID_MODE if this is called in PTRNG mode.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_NO_SEED if no seed passed for DRNG mode.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_UNNECESSARY_PARAM if seed passed for non-DRNG mode.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_RESEED_DFLENMUL if invalid DFLenMul parameter passed.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_SAME_SEED if same seed passed for both Instantiation and Reseed.
Other error codes from the called functions as defined in XTrngpsv_ErrorCodes.
Validate parameters
if initial seed during instantiation and reseed is same, it is an error
Call the actual reseed function

briefdescription.para: This the function which actually generates and provides random bits to the caller.

detaileddescription.para: Number of bits generated per call is 256 bits. If user needs more bits of random data, this API can be called multiple times accordingly.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to memory address where generated random data will be stored and the memory address should be word aligned.
is size of the buffer to which RandBufPtr points to and it should be always greater than or equal to XTRNGPSV_SEC_STRENGTH_BYTES.
is the flag that controls Generate level Prediction Resistance. When enabled, it mandates fresh seed for every Generate operation.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Random number generation was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter(s) passed to this function.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_STATE if driver is not Healthy state before invoking this.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INSUFFICIENT_RANDBUF if length of Buffer passed is insufficient.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_GEN_PREDRES if Prediction Resistance set for PTRNG mode.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_PREDRES_MISMATCH if Pred Resistance not set during Instantiate but set now.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_RESEEDING_REQUIRED if SeedLife elapsed.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_RESEED_REQD_PREDRES if seed is consumed and has Pred Resistance.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GLITCH if error caused due to glitch conditions.
Other error codes from the called functions as defined in XTrngpsv_ErrorCodes.
Validate parameters
If during Instantiation Prediction Resistance is enabled and not during Generate, its an error
Reseed if SeedLife elapsed
If prediction resistance enabled but seed is not new, reseed now
Error if SeedLife elapsed
If prediction resistance enabled but seed is not new, its an error
UsrCfg.Mode == XTRNGPSV_PTRNG
Fill the DFInput datastructure with 0s so that it can be populated
Enable all the 8 ring oscillators used for entropy source Provide soft reset, Enable loading entropy data as random number
Collect random data based on above configuration
In PTRNG mode with DF, collected random data to be fed as input of DF

briefdescription.para: This function is used to put the TRNG in reset state, and clear the instance data including configuration, status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Uninstantiation was successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter(s) passed to this function.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_STATE if driver is not Healthy state before invoking this.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GLITCH if error caused due to glitch conditions.
Validate arguments.
Clear contents of external seed and personalization string
Clear the instance datastructure

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XTrng_Config structure based on the DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xtrng_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being lookedup.

simplesect.para: XTrng_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xtrngpsv.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

listitem.para: Checks all the instances

briefdescription.para: This function implements the Derivative Function (per NIST SP80090A) by distilling the entropy available in a lot of bits at its input (DFInput) into a smaller number of bits on the output (DFOutput), thus bringing entropy per bit to 1.

detaileddescription.para: Actual length of input to DF need to be provided to the core (block cipher)algorithm for DF, based on if personalization string is present or not and based on actual length of input entropy, the buffer needs to adjusted by pushing all the unused bytes to the end. The Block Cipher algorithm invoked here is as per sections 10.3.2 and 10.3.3 of the NIST.SP.800-90Ar1 document.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.
points to the buffer to which output of DF operation is stored.
is the flag used to indicate if this function is used to generate seed or random number (used in the PTRNG mode)
is the pointer to Personalization string. If no personalization string is present, it should be NULL.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_DF_CPY if MemCpy failure
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_GLITCH if error caused due to glitch conditions.

EntropyData member of DFInputis already populated by calling functions, remaining members of this data structure to be filled here. Block cipher algorithm expects data to be present in a specific format which is the XTrngpsv_DFInput format
DF_RAND
Below is optional step just to make sure that remaining bytes (beyond what is given as input to DF) is cleared with 0s
Move the block up
Fill the remaining memory (after moving) with 0s
Perform first part of the DF algorithm
Perform second part of the DF algorithm (final update to DFOutput)
Clear the aes key schedule once DF operation is done

briefdescription.para: KAT can be run at startup and on demand.

detaileddescription.para: 256 bits are generated and ignored. This test can be run when the TRNG is configured in DRNG and HRNG modes. For HRNG mode too, tests should be run in DRNG mode. If KAT fails, driver has to be put in Error state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if KAT is successful.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter passed to this function.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_USRCFG_CPY_KAT if copy of seed/personalization string failed.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_KAT_MISMATCH if KAT result doesn't match with expected output (OR) other error codes from the called functions as defined in XTrngpsv_ErrorCodes.
Populate user config parameters of TRNGPSV driver
Invoke Instantiate (which includes reseed), (explicit) Reseed and Generate operations
If the generated Random data doesn't match with reference, error out.
Uninstantiate driver instance after KAT

briefdescription.para: Health tests are entropy tests and hence should be run when the configured mode is of PTRNG or HRNG mode.

detaileddescription.para: 1024 bits of entropy data to be generated and the CTF flag to be monitored. TRNG configured in HRNG + DF serves this purpose ((7+1)*128 = 1024 bits). Since this flag is monitored for every reseed, no need to monitor CTF explicitly in this test. This API is to be called at startup, after instantiation (and reseeding). Even for PTRNG mode, for this test, mode to be configured as HRNG (as there is no reseed operation involved in PTRNG mode).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTrngpsv instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XTRNGPSV_SUCCESS if Health tests are successfully run.
XTRNGPSV_ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if invalid parameter passed to this function (OR) other error codes from the called functions as defined in XTrngpsv_ErrorCodes.
Populate user config parameters of TRNGPSV driver
Invoke Instantiate (which includes reseed) operation
Status Reset
CTF is not being monitored explicitly here since that is done as part of Reseeding during Instantiation
Uninstantiate driver instance after Health Tests

detaileddescription.para: of SEED and PERS STRING registers each

briefdescription.para: Entropy SEED length in terms of security strength.
Shift value in terms of security strength.
Mask to check whether the address is word aligned.

detaileddescription.para: This assumes additional multiplier of 1 for nonce
of bytes per Generate request
of 32 bit words

briefdescription.para: to indicate DF called for seed
to indicate DF called for random number

detaileddescription.para: of bytes per block, equivalent to 128 bits

briefdescription.para: Length of Padding data used for DF calculation.
Boolean TRUE definition of TRNGPSV driver.
Boolean FALSE definition of TRNGPSV driver.
Macro to swap endianness of 32 bit data.
Value to be written to first byte of Padding data.
Contains the required functions of the XTrngpsv driver.

detaileddescription.para: Contains Known Answer Tests and Health Tests for the TRNGPSV component.
This file contains static initialization method for Xilinx TRNG driver.
This header file contains identifiers and register-level core functions (or macros) that can be used to access the True Random Number Generator core.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of TRNG in the system.
Implements the DF functionality, calls the functions that implement core algorithm for the DF implementation.
See xtrng.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 ssc 09/05/21 First release 1.1 ssc 03/24/22 Updates based on Security best practices and other assorted changes 1.2 kpt 08/03/22 Added volatile keyword to avoid compiler optimization of loop redundancy checks ssc 08/25/22 Updates based on Security best practices, error handling fix in XTrngpsv_Generate, moved Xil_SecureRMW32 to BSP. 1.3 kpt 01/31/23 Fixed RGRG sequence 1.4 ng 06/30/23 Added support for system device-tree flow 1.8 bha 01/07/26 Fixed Doxygen warnings

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 ssc 09/05/21 First release 1.1 ssc 03/24/22 Updates based on Security best practices 1.2 kpt 08/03/22 Added volatile keyword to avoid compiler optimization of loop redundancy check 1.3 kpt 01/31/23 Fixed issue when DF length is greater than 5 kpt 01/20/23 Clear key schedule array after DF operation is done

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 ssc 09/05/21 First release

detaileddescription.para: For more information about the operation of this core see the hardware specification and documentation in the higher level driver xtrngpsv.h file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 ssc 09/05/21 First release 1.1 ssc 03/24/22 Minor doxygen related fixes mb 09/14/23 Fix MISRA-C violation 2.5 1.5 kpt 03/06/24 Fix doxygen warnings

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 ssc 09/05/21 First release 1.01 ng 06/30/23 Added support for system device-tree flow

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 ssc 09/05/21 First release 1.1 ssc 03/24/22 Updates based on Security best practices 1.2 ssc 08/25/22 Enhanced KAT to include explicit Reseed and made a doxygen update
